oracle.commit: see scripts/sim/build-oracle.mjs TAG — this block had drifted and said v2 long after #69 re-pinned to v3; corrected 2026-07-13 (#90/#98)

lastRecord.kind: HARNESS RE-RECORD — the oracle bundle did NOT change, and neither did src/. This is not a re-pin. The SIMULATED GAME changed: it now runs its own trigger system, which it never had.
lastRecord.what: scripts/sim/driver.mjs tickOnce() now calls checkTriggers() after gameLoop(). boot.mjs stubs `window.setTimeout = () => 0` to suppress the game's self-driving loops, and the game calls checkTriggers() during play from exactly ONE place — costUpdatesTimeout(), a `setTimeout(costUpdatesTimeout, 250)` loop (main.js:17970). So it NEVER FIRED. Forge is a *trigger*, not an upgrade (config.js:13226, fires at >=350 metal): Forge never unlocked -> metal.max pinned at 500 on every save in the corpus -> Coordination (507 metal at done=2) permanently unaffordable. AutoTrimps bought Coordination ZERO times in the entire history of the proof net, and the deep fixtures ran metal-capped (owned === max === 500) on up to 100% of their ticks.
lastRecord.why: Every metal, gear, storage and housing conclusion the sim ever produced was measured against a DEAD economy — which is how #57 came to be designed against a premise ('Coordination gets starved by lesser spending') that was structurally unobservable in the simulator. This is #66 in a new costume: a stubbed environment silently disabling a whole game subsystem while every gate stayed green. Of costUpdatesTimeout's five calls only checkTriggers has game-state effect here — the four checkButtons() calls resolve to DOM class swaps (updateButtonColor) plus updateSRBuyAmt, which no-ops while usingScreenReader is false (boot sets it) — so cherry-picking it is shown, not assumed. Cadence is exact, not approximate: resources only move inside gameLoop, so a trigger can only become newly-affordable immediately after one.
lastRecord.honesty: THIS RE-RECORD IS NOT ADDITIVE, and unlike #90/#98 it cannot claim byte-identity. Counted BY EVENT (LCS over fn+args, not by index): 04-u2-radon reproduces BYTE-IDENTICAL (1204 -> 1204, 0 inserted / 0 deleted) — U2's economy is radon-based and never touches Forge, which is the control proving the change is inert where it should be. The shallow U1 saves move a little and entirely explicably (01-early: 33 -> 33, +buyBuilding(Forge), +buyUpgrade(Efficiency); 03-challenge: 30 -> 30, +Forge). The deep saves genuinely RESHAPE (06-deep: 1765 -> 2013 events, 303 inserted / 55 deleted; 08-starved: 1601 -> 1872, 649 in / 378 out), dominated by setFormation churn — the bot now has metal, buys more gear, and crosses its damage/health thresholds on different ticks. That is the intended consequence of a working economy, not drift, but it is a real trajectory change and is recorded here as one.
lastRecord.sensitivityCheck: REACH != SENSITIVITY (#98). 08-starved-u1 earns its place by leaving AT's damage threshold UNSATURATED, and a live economy buys more gear there (equipment levels 70 -> 79), which could in principle saturate it and quietly blunt the net. Re-verified after this re-record: tests/sim/damage-sensitivity.test.ts's POSITIVE control (1,000,000x damage injection) still goes RED against the fresh traces. If a future corpus change makes that control pass, the net has lost its ability to see combat regressions — fix the corpus, not the test.

supersededRecord.kind: ADDITIVE RE-RECORD — the oracle bundle did NOT change. This is not a re-pin.
supersededRecord.what: Added four corpus saves (05-maps-u1, 06-deep-u1, 07-map-cap-u1, 08-starved-u1) and two recorded mutators (buyMap, recycleBelow). Traces for the new saves were recorded fresh; traces for 01-04 were re-recorded and verified BYTE-IDENTICAL to their committed predecessors (all 10 files, cmp-clean). That byte-identity is the proof this was additive and not a laundered behavior change — it is the check to repeat on any future corpus growth.
supersededRecord.why: The net was structurally blind to the bot. It recorded only buy events, and every save decoded to world=4 with mapsUnlocked=false, so maps.ts:253 short-circuited calcOurDmg out of every decision: a 1,000,000x damage multiplier passed the whole sim suite GREEN (#98). Two DISTINCT fixes were needed, and the second is the one that is easy to miss. (1) REACH: the recorder was watching the wrong functions — AT creates maps via buyMap() (38 callsites) and mass-recycles via recycleBelow() (3), neither of which was wrapped, while the wrapped recycleMap is only the 100-map-cap fallback. Plus the corpus could not reach maps at all. (2) SATURATION: reach alone did NOT restore sensitivity. On 06 (deep, mapping, formations, calcOurDmg called every tick) the 1e6x injection STILL diffed to ZERO, because AT's damage decisions are threshold predicates — enoughDamage = (dmg * cutoff > enemyHealth) — and on 06 that predicate is already TRUE, so a buff cannot move it. 08-starved-u1 is damage-starved but perked, leaving the threshold UNSATURATED; the same injection now yields 1542 divergences there. Calling a function is not the same as depending on its answer. tests/sim/damage-sensitivity.test.ts makes this a permanent, self-checking mutation gate.

oracle.why: TWO reasons, both load-bearing. (1) HARNESS WAS BLIND: until #66, scripts/sim/boot.mjs left `usingRealTimeOffline` stuck true after load() — the game's offline-progress replay sets it (main.js:2901) and normally clears it via a setTimeout loop the sim stubs out. AT's mainLoop gates `setScienceNeeded()` + `autoLevelEquipment()` on `!usingRealTimeOffline`, so EVERY trace recorded against phase1-faithful was recorded with all gear-buying and science tracking disabled: zero buyEquipment events, U1 buyBuilding nearly absent. The L0 net was comparing a crippled AT against a crippled AT. (2) THE OLD ORACLE ENSHRINED A SHIPPED BUG: phase1-faithful contains #63 (needGymystic hardcoded true → scienceNeeded permanently +5,000,000). On 02-mid-u1 the old oracle computes scienceNeeded=5,001,452 and gathers science where the fixed build computes 1,452 and gathers buildings; every downstream buy timing cascades from that one number. The (save,index,fn) waiver mechanism is built for a few LOCALIZED fix divergences, not a wholly shifted trajectory (~130 brittle entries). Re-pinning was the correct move, and the v2 traces are strictly RICHER than the old ones (they contain the buyEquipment/buyBuilding events the old ones structurally could not), so the net is more sensitive after the re-pin than before it. NOTE: re-pinning is NOT routine — a naked oracle change is exactly the accidental-drift alarm this net exists to raise. Only re-pin behind a root-caused, reviewed, intentional behavior change.

notes.coverage: radon-only; the U1 corpus (01/02/03) never reaches RbuyJobs. The 04-u2-radon save (added #47) DOES run RbuyJobs — yet it produces ZERO L0 delta for THIS fixture's numeric window (verified 2026-07-10): L0 records buyJob(name, buyMax, force), and for a normal buy the fix only shifts the worker QUANTITY computed internally, not buyJob's args (buyMax=true carries no count). NOTE this is empirical, NOT a structural invariant: freeWorkers (jobs.ts:492) also feeds an `if (freeWorkers<=0) return` gate BEFORE any buyJob call, so a different U2 save/seed whose fixed-vs-buggy freeWorkers straddles 0 WOULD surface a real L0 divergence (missing buyJob calls that tick). For 04-u2-radon it stays well above the gate, so the fix remains gated by the jobs.RbuyJobs L1 regression (156→33), not an L0 waiver. This corrects the earlier prediction that adding a U2 save would force an L0 waiver here.
